title: <div><tt>  - Read or skim core NY text units on property ownership, estates, and
content:
- Read or skim core NY text units on property ownership, estates, and basic contract law (roughly first 3–4 chapters of a NY text).[13][14][1]
- Listen daily to 30–40 minutes of matching audio (AudioLearn or Modern RE Practice key‑point MP3s) while commuting/walking.[4][5][3]
- Days 4–6:
- Cover agency, brokerage relationships, and fiduciary duties in NY.
- Do 25–40 mixed questions per day on these topics from Barron’s or your chosen app, reviewing every explanation carefully.[7][8][12][6]
- End‑of‑week target:
- Score at least 70% on short quizzes for ownership + agency; note any weak subtopics in a notebook.

***

## Week 2 – NY law, finance, and math

Goal: Refresh NY‑specific law, financing, and calculations.

- Days 1–3:
- Study NY license law, prohibited practices, fair housing (especially state‑level details), and disclosure rules from the NY text or course.[14][15][1][13]
context: From document: Since she has prior experience and is coming back after a lapse, this… — chunk 2 (chunk 2/8)
